diary:
Started the day slow with coffee and a good walk around Eastlake Park—the overcast sky felt perfect, honestly. No rush. Drove up to Ballard this morning and spent a couple hours at the farmers market, chatting with the same vendors I always see. Picked up gorgeous heirloom tomatoes and some fresh bread. Grabbed an espresso at Vivace on the way back because, why not?

Lunch was simple—just chopped up what I'd bought and sat with it. Then I spent the afternoon with my sourdough starter, which has been behaving beautifully. There's something meditative about folding dough, watching it rise. No Mariners game for me today, even with all that SoDo buzz.

The highlight was salsa dancing with Claire at the studio in Capitol Hill. My hips remembered what my brain forgot—it's been too long. Ended the night with ice cream and a quiet drive home. Tired now, but good tired.
thought_process: I made deliberate choices today to keep things grounded. The weather report showed overcast conditions, which honestly lifted my mood—I don't need sunshine to feel good. I skipped the farmers markets in Fremont and U-District even though they were busy; Ballard felt right, quieter, more my pace at 48. The sourdough baking wasn't random either; I've been stressed lately and needed that tactile, slow work. Salsa dancing was non-negotiable because I promised Claire weeks ago, and frankly, I needed to move my body and laugh. The Mariners crowds and downtown energy didn't pull at me today—I was already oriented toward the neighborhood, the intimate, the handmade. Sometimes you need a Saturday that's about quality over novelty.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
